w:r.w:t: A Data-Driven Platform for Bollywood Movie Investment Intelligence
Asad Ali Khan (Roll No. 36) | Harshad Karne (Roll No. 35)
Department of Information Technology, SAKEC, Mumbai

CineIntel is a full-stack AI-powered web application that provides data-driven investment intelligence for Bollywood movies. It uses machine learning (Random Forest Classifier) to predict film profitability and provides interactive dashboards for genre analysis, financial risk assessment, and investment simulation.
The application consists of two main components:
Backend: Python FastAPI server running on Port 8000
Frontend: Next.js 14 React application running on Port 3000
Before installing CineIntel, ensure you have the following software installed on your system:
Locate the submitted ZIP file: CineIntel_Project_Submission.zip
Extract the ZIP file to your desired location (e.g., C:\Projects\CineIntel or ~/Projects/CineIntel)
You will see the following folder structure after extraction:
Reports/ <- Technical report, IEEE paper, Turnitin
Open a Terminal (Mac/Linux) or Command Prompt/PowerShell (Windows) and run:
If you have both Python 2 and Python 3, use pip3 instead of pip.
INFO: Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
The backend server must remain running while using the application.
Open a NEW terminal window and run:
Open any modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge recommended)
The CineIntel dashboard should load automatically
Navigate to the Installable/ folder in the submission
The browser will automatically open at http://localhost:3000
If you have Docker and Docker Compose installed, you can run the entire application with a single command:
Build and run with Docker Compose:
After starting both servers, verify everything is working:
Open browser and go to: http://localhost:8000/docs
You should see the FastAPI Swagger UI with all available endpoints
Open browser and go to: http://localhost:3000
The Executive Dashboard should display market sentiment and KPI metrics
Navigate through the sidebar: Movie Explorer, Genre Intelligence, Financial Risk, Investment Simulator
ROI benchmarks, hit rates by genre
If port 8000 or 3000 is already in use:
Try using pip3 instead of pip
Ensure Python is in your system PATH
Delete node_modules folder and package-lock.json, then run npm install again
Ensure Node.js version is 16 or higher: node --version
Verify backend server is running (uvicorn must be active)
Ensure no firewall is blocking port 8000
Pandas and NumPy for data processing
For technical support, contact: harshad.karne24@sakec.ac.in | asadali.khan24@sakec.ac.in
